originalTitle: Chip titan SK hynix to raise $26.5 bn in blockbuster US listing
summary: South Korean chip giant SK hynix on Friday priced its mega US listing at $149 per share, raising around $26.5 billion in what will be the world's second largest stock sale after SpaceX. As governments and tech firms race to build data centres to train and run artificial intelligence, SK hynix -- a supplier to industry behemoth Nvidia -- has seen profits and its share price skyrocket.Global tech stocks have tumbled in recent weeks amid fears about extended valuations -- SK hynix has jumped more t
content:
South Korean chip giant SK hynix on Friday priced its mega US listing at $149 per share, raising around $26.5 billion in what will be the world's second largest stock sale after SpaceX.

As governments and tech firms race to build data centres to train and run artificial intelligence, SK hynix -- a supplier to industry behemoth Nvidia -- has seen profits and its share price skyrocket.

The Asian semiconductor giant plans to issue the equivalent of nearly 18 million new shares on Wall Street's tech-heavy Nasdaq index on Friday through something called American Depositary Shares, which allow slices of foreign companies to be traded on US public markets.

SK hynix said this week it hoped to raise a whopping 43 trillion won or $28 billion, but recent volatility in the company's shares had apparently scaled back the target.

Global tech stocks have tumbled in recent weeks amid fears about extended valuations -- SK hynix has jumped more than 200 percent this year -- and questions over when the enormous spending on artificial intelligence will reap returns.

The amount raised did not come close to the record $75 billion raised in SpaceX's IPO last month, which made founder Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire.

But it beat out Saudi Aramco's 2019 $25.6 billion debut in the Gulf, and the $21.8 billion raised by Chinese tech firm Alibaba in its New York IPO.

Ahead of the pricing, Dilin Wu, research strategist at Pepperstone, told AFP the listing was "a huge development that should broaden the capital base for the memory sector".

SK hynix is already traded on Seoul's Kospi index, where its market capitalisation soared past $1 trillion in May.

The milestone was also recently hit by domestic rival Samsung Electronics and US chipmaker Micron -- with the AI boom thrusting the three semiconductor firms into a previously exclusive club of around a dozen companies, nearly all American.

Samsung, SK hynix and Micron dominate the global market for the advanced components known as high-bandwidth memory (HBM), used in AI servers alongside other data-crunching semiconductors.

But as chipmakers plough resources into lucrative HBM, shortages of the less flashy memory chips in everyday consumer electronics are pushing up prices, with Apple hiking the cost of its MacBooks and iPads.

Counterpoint Research analyst MS Hwang said SK hynix wants to triumph over Samsung in the red-hot memory chip market.

"Along with the HBM leadership it has demonstrated until recently, the company is now planning to take the lead in terms of volume as well," Hwang told AFP.

"The use of funds from its US listing can support such a goal," Hwang said.

The AI chip boom has fuelled debate over what South Korea should do with the tax windfall, as well as workers' demands over pay packages -- with Samsung averting a strike by agreeing a deal on bonuses.

An image of an SK hynix jacket also went viral online this year as a symbol of wealth and success, with parody posts depicting it as a "golden ticket" to luxury boutiques or better dating prospects.

SK hynix has said proceeds from the offering will be used to fund new chip factories to ease shortages.

The company, along with Samsung, is involved in a massive public-private investment of 800 trillion won to build a new semiconductor fabrication hub in southwest South Korea.
chineseTitle: 芯片巨头 SK 海力士将在美国上市，筹集 265 亿美元资金
chineseSummary: 韩国芯片巨头 SK 海力士周五在美国上市的定价为每股 149 美元，筹集了约 265 亿美元，这将是仅次于 SpaceX 的全球第二大股票发行。随着政府和科技公司竞相建设数据中心来训练和运行人工智能，行业巨头英伟达的供应商 SK 海力士的利润和股价飙升。 由于担心估值扩大，全球科技股近几周暴跌，SK 海力士的涨幅更大。
chineseContent:
韩国芯片巨头 SK 海力士周五在美国上市的定价为每股 149 美元，筹集了约 265 亿美元，这将是仅次于 SpaceX 的全球第二大股票发行。

随着政府和科技公司竞相建设数据中心来训练和运行人工智能，行业巨头英伟达的供应商 SK 海力士的利润和股价都在飙升。

这家亚洲半导体巨头计划于周五通过美国存托股票在华尔街以科技股为主的纳斯达克指数中发行相当于近 1800 万股新股，该股票允许部分外国公司在美国公开市场进行交易。

SK 海力士本周表示，希望筹集高达 43 万亿韩元（即 280 亿美元）的资金，但该公司股价近期的波动显然缩减了这一目标。

最近几周，由于人们担心估值扩大（SK 海力士今年的股价上涨了 200% 以上），以及对人工智能的巨额支出何时能获得回报的疑问，全球科技股大幅下挫。

筹集的金额远低于 SpaceX 上个月 IPO 筹集的创纪录的 750 亿美元，后者使创始人埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 成为世界上第一位万亿富翁。

但它击败了沙特阿美公司 2019 年在海湾地区首次公开募股 256 亿美元，以及中国科技公司阿里巴巴在纽约 IPO 筹集的 218 亿美元。

在定价之前，Pepperstone 研究策略师 Dilin Wu 告诉法新社，此次上市是“一个巨大的发展，应该会扩大存储行业的资本基础”。

SK 海力士已在首尔 Kospi 指数上市，5 月份其市值飙升至 1 万亿美元以上。

最近，国内竞争对手三星电子和美国芯片制造商美光科技也实现了这一里程碑——人工智能的繁荣将这三家半导体公司推入了之前由十几家公司组成的专属俱乐部，而这些公司几乎都是美国公司。

三星、SK 海力士和美光在高带宽内存 (HBM) 先进组件的全球市场上占据主导地位，这些组件与其他数据处理半导体一起用于人工智能服务器。

但随着芯片制造商将资源投入利润丰厚的 HBM，日常消费电子产品中不太华丽的存储芯片的短缺正在推高价格，苹果提高了 MacBook 和 iPad 的成本。

Counterpoint Research分析师MS Hwang表示，SK海力士希望在炙手可热的存储芯片市场战胜三星。

黄告诉法新社：“除了最近才展现出的 HBM 领导地位外，该公司现在还计划在销量方面占据领先地位。”

“利用其在美国上市的资金可以支持这一目标，”黄说。

人工智能芯片的繁荣引发了关于韩国应如何处理这笔意外之财以及工人对薪资待遇的要求的争论——三星通过就奖金达成协议避免了罢工。

今年，一张 SK 海力士夹克的图片也在网上疯传，被视为财富和成功的象征，还有一些模仿帖子将其描述为通往豪华精品店或更好约会前景的“金券”。

SK海力士表示，此次发行的收益将用于资助新的芯片工厂，以缓解短缺问题。

该公司与三星一起参与了 800 万亿韩元的大规模公私投资，在韩国西南部建设一个新的半导体制造中心。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
